purpose: Professional Git workflow practices: atomic commits, Conventional Commits, branching strategy, staging, history rewriting, tagging, hygiene, and pull request workflow.

documents.title: Git Best Practices
documents.description: Comprehensive guide to atomic commits, conventional commits, branching strategy, staging, history rewriting, tagging, hygiene, PR workflow, and common mistakes in professional Git usage.

terminal_objective.prerequisite: Basic git commands (init, add, commit, push, pull, branch, merge)
terminal_objective.concept: Professional Git usage is a system of conventions — atomic commits, structured messages, principled branching, clean history, and collaborative review — that makes a codebase's change history a first-class engineering artifact: readable, debuggable, and deployable at any point.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Author an atomic commit with a Conventional Commits message; rebase an in-progress feature branch interactively to squash and reword commits before opening a PR; configure a pre-commit hook that runs lint and type-check; create an annotated tag following semantic versioning; write a PR description that links a closing issue.
terminal_objective.market_value: High — professional Git hygiene is universally expected at all experience levels; Conventional Commits is the standard in OSS and enterprise CI/CD pipelines; recruiters and hiring managers read commit histories during portfolio review.

educational_asset_templates.title: Atomic Commit Workshop
educational_asset_templates.description: Given a dirty working tree with three mixed changes, use git add -p to craft three separate atomic commits with correct Conventional Commits messages.
educational_asset_templates.deliverable: Three commits visible in git log --oneline: one feat, one fix, one refactor — each containing only the relevant hunks

educational_asset_templates.title: Interactive Rebase Cleanup
educational_asset_templates.description: Given a branch with five WIP commits, use git rebase -i to squash them into two clean, well-named commits before opening a pull request.
educational_asset_templates.deliverable: A clean two-commit history with properly formatted Conventional Commits messages, pushed to a feature branch

educational_asset_templates.title: Release Tagging and Changelog
educational_asset_templates.description: Apply Conventional Commits retroactively to a small project, tag a v1.0.0 release with an annotated tag, and generate a CHANGELOG.md using standard-version or release-please.
educational_asset_templates.deliverable: An annotated v1.0.0 tag pushed to remote; a generated CHANGELOG.md committed to the repository
